title: Arrival Camp
question: Who am I?
purpose: Welcome the traveller and introduce the developer's identity before the journey begins.
environment: A cozy clearing at first light, a campfire crackling beside a small tent as mist lifts off the path ahead.

dialogue.speaker: Wren, the Guide
lines: Welcome, traveller. I'm Wren — I tend this little camp at the start of the road.
Everything ahead is the story of one developer, told as a place you can walk through.
Follow the lanterns and firepits; they mark the way when the path bends out of sight.
Take your time. Use WASD or the arrow keys to walk, and I'll be here when you wander back.

transition: When you're ready, follow the path out of camp toward the sound of hammering — the workshop is waiting.

title: The Workshop
question: What have I built?
purpose: Show the things the developer has made and how they were put together.
environment: A canvas workshop tent ringed by workbenches and half-finished prototypes, tools resting where they were last used.

dialogue.speaker: Wren, the Guide
lines: This is the Workshop — where ideas get hammered into things that actually work.
Look around: every bench holds a project that started as a rough sketch.
Nothing here is precious. It's all made to be taken apart and rebuilt better.

transition: Past the workbenches, a quiet building of shelves rises through the trees — the Library is next.

title: The Library
question: What do I know?
purpose: Reveal the developer's skills and the learning that grew them.
environment: A grove built around a great tree, its branches hung with lanterns and its roots stacked with weathered books.

dialogue.speaker: Wren, the Guide
lines: The Library — knowledge grows tall here, a ring at a time.
Some of these books are languages, some are tools, some are hard lessons learned twice.
Skills aren't shelved and forgotten; they're tended like the tree that holds them.

transition: A faint hum and a glow lead off the main path toward a low building of glass and wire — the AI Laboratory.

title: The AI Laboratory
question: What am I exploring?
purpose: Surface the experimental, forward-looking work the developer is curious about right now.
environment: A glass-walled lab humming with prototypes and soft indicator lights, screens sketching ideas that aren't finished yet.

dialogue.speaker: Wren, the Guide
lines: The AI Laboratory — this is where the half-formed ideas live.
Not everything here will ship. That's the point; it's a place to try and to fail quickly.
The experiments that survive tend to wander back to the Workshop one day.

transition: Beyond the lab the ground rises toward a domed silhouette against the sky — climb on to the Observatory.

title: The Observatory
question: How do I think?
purpose: Explain the developer's way of working and reasoning across their experience.
environment: A stone rise crowned with an obelisk and a charting table, the whole valley laid out below for planning the road ahead.

dialogue.speaker: Wren, the Guide
lines: The Observatory — for charting where the work goes next.
From up here you can see how the pieces of a career line up over time.
Good thinking is mostly stepping back far enough to see the whole map.

transition: One last light waits at the end of the road, steady over the water — walk on to the Lighthouse.

title: The Lighthouse
question: Why should someone work with me?
purpose: Close the journey with the value of collaborating with the developer and how to reach them.
environment: A tall lighthouse at the path's end, its beam sweeping out over calm water as a steady signal for those looking to connect.

dialogue.speaker: Wren, the Guide
lines: The Lighthouse — a steady signal for collaborators to find.
Working together should feel calm: clear trade-offs, simple systems, docs you can actually follow.
If the light reached you, follow it home — the links here are how you say hello.

transition: That's the whole road, traveller. Wander back to camp whenever you like — Wren will be waiting by the fire.
